name: American Interstate Bank
description_short: Community-focused bank chartered in Elkhorn, Nebraska in 1915, offering personal and business banking with access to 25,000 nationwide ATMs through Money Pass Network.
description_long:
American Interstate Bank (AIB) was founded in 1915 and remains the only bank chartered in Elkhorn, Nebraska. The institution is wholly owned by American Interstate Bancorporation, headquartered in Omaha, Nebraska. Over its 110+ year history, AIB has maintained deep roots in the Elkhorn and Western Douglas County communities through involvement in service organizations, churches, schools, and community projects.

AIB offers a full range of banking products and services for both personal and business customers. The bank operates two physical branch locations: the Elkhorn Branch at 3331 N. 204th Street and the Omaha Branch at 15909 West Maple Road. Customers also have access to online banking for account management and can utilize mobile banking through the AIB App. Beyond physical branches, AIB customers can access any of the 25,000 Money Pass ATM Network locations across the United States, plus an in-network ATM at a local Hy-Vee store location.

AIB's primary distinguishing feature is its ultra-local community focus and 110-year institutional history as Elkhorn's original bank. The bank emphasizes personal relationships and community involvement rather than competing on digital innovation or product breadth. Their extended ATM network through Money Pass provides significant cash access parity with larger national banks despite limited branch footprint.

For consumers seeking traditional community banking with personal service and local decision-making, AIB is a legitimate option. However, the bank's limited digital information disclosure on its website, lack of detailed product specifications (rates, fees, minimum balances), and geographic concentration in the Omaha metro area restrict its appeal to broader audiences. The absence of information about specific products, APRs, or service fees makes direct comparison with competitors difficult.
services: Online banking platform with account access
Mobile banking via AIB App (iOS and Android)
In-person banking at Elkhorn branch (3331 N. 204th Street, Elkhorn, NE)
In-person banking at Omaha branch (15909 West Maple Road, Suite 100, Omaha, NE)
Access to 25,000 Money Pass ATM locations nationally
In-network ATM at Hy-Vee store location (3410 N. 156th Street, Omaha, NE)
Drive-up banking services at both branches
Bank and ATM location finder services
pros: Access to 25,000 Money Pass ATM locations nationwide despite only 2 physical branches
110+ year track record as original and only bank chartered in Elkhorn, Nebraska
Mobile banking app available for both iOS and Android platforms
Online banking portal for account access and management
Extended lobby and drive-up hours at Elkhorn branch (including Saturday mornings)
Dual locations in Elkhorn and Omaha covering Western Douglas County
Wholly owned by bank holding company, indicating stable local ownership structure
cons: Website lacks specific product details including interest rates, APYs, fees, or minimum balance requirements
Limited to 2 physical branch locations with Saturday service only at Elkhorn branch
Geographic service area restricted to Elkhorn and Omaha area—not suitable for customers outside Nebraska
No information provided about deposit insurance limits, loan products, or business banking specifics
Website appears dated (copyright 2018) with minimal product information compared to peer banks
best_for: Elkhorn and Western Douglas County residents seeking personalized community banking relationships
Small business owners in the Omaha metro area needing local decision-makers
Customers who prioritize long-term institutional stability and local roots over digital innovation
diagnosis: American Interstate Bank is best for existing or prospective residents of the Elkhorn/Omaha area who value community banking, local decision-making, and personal relationships over modern digital features. The main caveat is the bank's limited geographic footprint and lack of transparent product information online—customers must contact branches directly to compare rates and fees with national competitors.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
